name: oslo-rootwrap

metadata.name: oslo.rootwrap
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Team and repository tags

```{image} https://governance.openstack.org/tc/badges/oslo.rootwrap.svg
:target: https://governance.openstack.org/tc/reference/tags/index.html
```

% Change things from this point on

# oslo.rootwrap -- Escalated Permission Control

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/oslo.rootwrap.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/oslo.rootwrap/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/oslo.rootwrap.svg
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pypi.org/project/oslo.rootwrap/
```

oslo.rootwrap allows fine-grained filtering of shell commands to run
as `root` from OpenStack services.

- License: Apache License, Version 2.0
- Documentation: <https://docs.openstack.org/oslo.rootwrap/latest/>
- Source: <https://opendev.org/openstack/oslo.rootwrap>
- Bugs: <https://bugs.launchpad.net/oslo.rootwrap>
- Release notes: <https://docs.openstack.org/releasenotes/oslo.rootwrap/>

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.client.Client._run_one_command
documentation:
Wrap proxy.run_one_command, setting _need_restart on an exception.

Usually it should be enough to drain stale data on socket
rather than to restart, but we cannot do draining easily.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.tests.test_functional_eventlet.RootwrapDaemonTest.test_eventlet_threads
documentation:
Check eventlet compatibility.

The multiprocessing module is not eventlet friendly and
must be protected against eventlet thread switching and its
timeout exceptions.

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.jsonrpc.rpc_object_hook
documentation: Parse whatever RpcJSONEncoder supplied us with

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.jsonrpc.silent_accepter
documentation: In Python 3 accepter() thread has infinite loop. We break it with
EOFError, so we should silence this error here.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.jsonrpc.JsonConnection._recvall_slow
documentation: We have to use slow version of recvall with eventlet because of a bug in
GreenSocket.recv_into:
https://bitbucket.org/eventlet/eventlet/pull-request/41

modules.name: oslo_rootwrap.cmd
documentation:
Root wrapper for OpenStack services

Filters which commands a service is allowed to run as another user.

To use this with oslo, you should set the following in
oslo.conf:
rootwrap_config=/etc/oslo/rootwrap.conf

You also need to let the oslo user run oslo-rootwrap
as root in sudoers:
oslo ALL = (root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/oslo-rootwrap
                                /etc/oslo/rootwrap.conf *

Service packaging should deploy .filters files only on nodes where
they are needed, to avoid allowing more than is necessary.

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.wrapper.build_filter
documentation: Returns a filter object of class class_name.

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.wrapper.load_filters
documentation: Load filters from a list of directories.

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.wrapper.match_filter
documentation:
Checks user command and arguments through command filters.

Returns the first matching filter.

Raises NoFilterMatched if no filter matched.
Raises FilterMatchNotExecutable if no executable was found for the
best filter match.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.wrapper.NoFilterMatched
documentation: This exception is raised when no filter matched.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.wrapper.FilterMatchNotExecutable
documentation: Raised when a filter matched but no executable was found.

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters._getuid
documentation: Return uid for user.

functions.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.realpath
documentation:
Return the real absolute path.

If the execution directory does not exist, os.getcwd() raises a
FileNotFoundError exception. In this case, unset the exception and return
an empty string.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.CommandFilter

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.CommandFilter.get_exec
documentation: Returns existing executable, or empty string if none found.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.CommandFilter.match
documentation: Only check that the first argument (command) matches exec_path.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.CommandFilter.preexec
documentation: Setuid in subprocess right before command is invoked.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.CommandFilter.get_command
documentation: Returns command to execute.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.CommandFilter.get_environment
documentation: Returns specific environment to set, None if none.
Command filter only checking that the 1st argument matches exec_path.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.RegExpFilter
documentation: Command filter doing regexp matching for every argument.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.PathFilter
documentation:
Command filter checking that path arguments are within given dirs

One can specify the following constraints for command arguments:
    1) pass     - pass an argument as is to the resulting command
    2) some_str - check if an argument is equal to the given string
    3) abs path - check if a path argument is within the given base dir

A typical rootwrapper filter entry looks like this:
    # cmdname: filter name, raw command, user, arg_i_constraint [, ...]
    chown: PathFilter, /bin/chown, root, nova, /var/lib/images

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.KillFilter

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.KillFilter._program_path
documentation:
Try to determine the full path for command.

Return command if the full path cannot be found.

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.KillFilter._program
documentation: Determine the program associated with pid

Specific filter for the kill calls.

1st argument is the user to run /bin/kill under
2nd argument is the location of the affected executable
    if the argument is not absolute, it is checked against $PATH
Subsequent arguments list the accepted signals (if any)

This filter relies on /proc to accurately determine affected
executable, so it will only work on procfs-capable systems (not OSX).

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.ReadFileFilter
documentation: Specific filter for the utils.read_file_as_root call.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.IpFilter
documentation: Specific filter for the ip utility to that does not match exec.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.EnvFilter

methods.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.EnvFilter._extract_env
documentation: Extract all leading NAME=VALUE arguments from arglist.

Specific filter for the env utility.

Behaves like CommandFilter, except that it handles
leading env A=B.. strings appropriately.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.IpNetnsExecFilter
documentation: Specific filter for the ip utility to that does match exec.

classes.name: oslo_rootwrap.filters.ChainingRegExpFilter
documentation:
Command filter doing regexp matching for prefix commands.

Remaining arguments are filtered again. This means that the command
specified as the arguments must be also allowed to execute directly.
